rulings.Case Title: SHELLEY WIENKE, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF THE VS. CHASE HOME FINANCE, LLC., A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Granting Leave Of Court To File A Fourth Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF SHELLEY WIENKE'S MOTION For Order Granting Leave Of Court To File A Fourth Amended Complaint. Granted in part. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to file a Fourth Amended Complaint except that Plaintiff is not given leave to amend to add the proposed eleventh cause of action for Violation of the Homeowner's Bill of Rights. As Plaintiff concedes, the Homeowner's Bill of Rights did not come into effect until January 2013 which is after the facts alleged in the proposed Fourth Amended Complaint took place. Before filing the Fourth Amended Complaint, Plaintiff should make any necessary modifications so that it conforms to the Court's June 21, 2013 ruling sustaining in part Defendant's demurrer to the Third Amended Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RIVER PROPERTY TRUST VS. DAWN WIGGINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Otion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013, Line 4, CROSS COMPLAINANT DAWN WIGGINS' MOTION For Attorneys Fees And Costs. Defendant and Cross-Complainant Wiggins' motion for prevailing party attorney's fees is denied without prejudice. The "prevailing party determination is to be made only upon final resolution of the contract claims." (See Hsu v. Abarra (1995) 9 Cal.4th 863, 876.) See also Civil Code §1717. Wiggins' motion is premature since there has not been a final resolution of the contract claims between the parties. Wiggins' Cross-Complaint filed September 11, 2012 remains outstanding, and there has not been a final resolution of the contract claims between the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE WILLIS VS. TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, July 3, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiff STEPHANIE WILLIS' Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for a protective order is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Plaintiff alleges for the first time in her third-amended complaint alleges at Paragraph 16 that, "Defendant's managing agents and employees had a subjective belief that Plaintiff was so upset by their actions that she might harm herself. Yet Defendant's agenst proceeded with their actions . . ." Moreover, Plaintiff alleges new facts in Paragraphs 13, 18, 19, and 21 of the Third Ameded Complaint in support her new cause of action for sex discrimination in violation of Gov't Code Section 12940 et seq. Defendant may depose plaintiff again for a maximum of 2 hours. The deposition shall be limited in subject matter to Plaintiff's claim for emotional distress and the new factual allegations in Paragraphs 13, 16, 18, 19 and 21 of Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint. Counsel for the parties are to meet and confer on a mutually agreeable date for the two-hour deposition before July 12th. Both parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP A NEW VS. JOAN MACK et al

rulings.Case Title: KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP A NEW VS. JOAN MACK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013, Line 9, PLAINTIFF KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Plaintiff's Motion to Strike is denied in part and granted in part. Plaintiff's Motion to Strike the entire Second Amended Cross-Complaint is denied. The Kaufman Declaration sets forth facts which constitute excusable neglect and there is no indication that Defendant's counsel acted deliberately instead of inadvertently in failing to timely file the second amended cross-complaint. The motion to strike is granted to the extent that the Second Amended Cross-Complaint includes a ninth cause of action for Penal Code §496(c). This cause of action is outside of the scope of the Court order filed January 29, 2013 which sustained in part and overruled in part Kasowitz's demurrer to the First Amended Cross-Complaint. The motion to strike is thus granted as to paragraphs 43 at lines 8-12, 72, 96, 123, 154, 177, 191, 200, 207(h), 207(i), 208-227, 262-267, 274. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JUVENTINO ESTRADA VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order To Compel Deposition Of Deft National Union Fire Insurance Company'S Person(S) Most Knowledgeable; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,250
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, July 3, 2013, Line 2: Plaintiff JUVENTINO ESTRADA'S Motion for Order to Compel Deposition of Defendant National Union Fire Insurance Company's Person(s) Most Knowledgeable; Request for Sanctions in the Amount of $2,250 Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Code of Civil Procedure 2025.250(d) mandates that the deposition of an organization that has not designated a principal place of business in California, "shall be taken at a place that is, at the option of the party giving notice of the deposition, either within the county where the action is pending, or within 75 miles of any executive or business office in California of the organization." Within 30 days Defendant shall produce for deposition in San Francisco those of its officers, directors, managing agents, employees, or agents who are most qualified to testify on its behalf as to the matters designated in Plaintiff's Notice of Deposition of Defendant National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburgh, PA's Person(s) Most knowledgeable re: Deliver of Policy, served on April 22, 2013, and shall produce the documents designated in Exhibit A to Plaintiff's Notice. Both parties' requests for sancitons are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL MORRIS PRODUCTIONS, INC. VS. GARY WEGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion From Relief From Default Entry
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT GARY WEGER'S Motion From Relief From Default Entry. The motion for relief from default is granted pursuant to CCP 473. Defendant provided a proposed answer with this motion. Defendant filed this motion in a timely manner. Defendant learned of the default in April 2013 and filed the motion for relief from default a month later. Defendant demonstrates excusable neglect in failing to respond to the complaint. Only slight evidence is necessary for relief because plaintiff is not prejudiced. See generally Mink v. Superior Court, 2 Cal. App. 4th 1338, 343 (1992). In addition, there is a policy to hear cases on the merits. The request for conditions to grant this motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, July 3, 2013, Line 3: Defendant DIGNITY HEALTH, INC.'S Motion to Deem the Truth of the Matters Specified in the Request for Admission, Set Two, Admitted; to Compel Verified Responses to Request for Production of Documents, Set Two, Without Objection; and Request for Attorneys' Fees and Costs Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The proof of service submitted with Defendant Dignith Health, Inc.'s memorandum of points and authorities in support of its motion shows that the memorandum of points and authorities was not served on plaintiff (who is currently in pro-per), but rather on his former counsel whose motion to withdraw was previously granted by the court. rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS BLASGEN VS. REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Reconsider Court Order Granting Deft'S Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013, Line 17, PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS BLASGEN'S Motion To Reconsider Court Order Granting DefENDANt'S Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration. The motion is denied without prejudice to bringing a motion pursuant to CCP 473 to have the Petition to Compel Arbitration reheard. Plaintiff files a proof of service of this motion in his untimely declaration filed July 1, 2013, but the proof of service itself indicates that the motion to reconsider was untimely served. CCP 1005 requires an additional five days for mail service. Further, the declaration of Mr. Signorotti filed in opposition to the motion states that service of this motion was late. The motion to reconsider does not comply with the requirements for CCP §1008. Further, without a copy of the underlying papers, the Court cannot address the merits of Plaintiff's arguments. The Court denies the sanctions request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. ALLEN LEE PLUMBING CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-520385 WITH CASE CGC-13-530490
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANTS ALLEN LEE, TT LEE CORPORATION DBA ALLEN LEE'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-520385 WITH CASE CGC-13-530490. Granted, for good cause shown. No opposition filed. The cases are consolidated for all purposes. The lowest number case (CGC-12-520385) is the lead case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
